title: DeepPartial<T> — recursive mapped type with nested optionality
description: `type DeepPartial<T> = {[K in keyof T]?: T[K] extends object ? DeepPartial<T[K]> : T[K]}`. Recursively makes every nested object-typed property optional. The resolver evaluates the recursion at the type-checker layer; the validator sees the fully flattened all-optional-deep shape.
notes: Every nested object becomes all-optional. The `allOptionalCode` guard fires at every level so non-plain-object inputs (arrays, Date, …) are rejected even though the all-optional shape would otherwise accept them.
generated:
function YOw_NtNPpTl(v){return (typeof v === 'object' && v !== null && (!Array.isArray(v) && Object.prototype.toString.call(v) === '[object Object]') && (v.volume === undefined || Number.isFinite(v.volume)) && (v.muted === undefined || (typeof v.muted === 'undefined' || v.muted === false || v.muted === true)))}

function YOw_p9RXJP5(v){return (typeof v === 'object' && v !== null && (!Array.isArray(v) && Object.prototype.toString.call(v) === '[object Object]') && (v.theme === undefined || (typeof v.theme === 'undefined' || v.theme === 'dark' || v.theme === 'light')) && (v.brightness === undefined || Number.isFinite(v.brightness)))}
